reason: Independent cross-language verification found fail-open omissions after the immutable G1 tag was published. The tag remains the common branch root; these corrections are mandatory release deltas and may not be relaxed by either lane.

rules: Every raw contract-step finality observation carries its validated block_hash and block_height into the verifier result.
Ordered contract-step block heights must be nondecreasing.
The state readback observed_block_height must be greater than or equal to the successful finalize_exact block_height.
A self-consistent state-readback artifact captured before finalize_exact is invalid even when its hashes and state fields otherwise verify.
Every public proof item contains deployment_domain, nullable for proof types without a v3 deployment identity.
A verified exact_envelope_v3 or native_treasury_execution_v1 item requires deployment_domain as lowercase 32-byte hex.
Two-pass exact-envelope and treasury verification requires exact equality of proposal_id, action_id, envelope_hash, network, package_hash, contract_hash, and deployment_domain.
Treasury temporal binding is snapshot_block_height < exact_v3_finalization_block_height == treasury_authorization_block_height <= native_execution_block_height.
Every contract-step finality block height is strictly greater than the installation block height.
Consecutive contract-step observations at an equal height must carry the identical canonical block hash.
A state readback at the exact-finalization height must carry the identical canonical block hash as finalize_exact.
The bounded native-transfer scan exposes the canonical hash observed at authorization_block_height.
The scan authorization block hash must equal the exact-v3 finalization block hash as well as matching its height.
A treasury execution on a competing block at the same height is invalid.
The humanized public evidence view is not a cryptographic card-hash preimage.
Independent card verification consumes the exact stored canonical_card_json bytes for each immutable historical card.
The adapter recomputes every card hash and previous-card link and ignores asserted chain-validity booleans.
The exact frozen preimages pass a secret scan before public release and are never modified during publication.

The producer requires a strict immutable release-root mapping generated only from the verified historical receipt final_card_hash; missing, request-selected, Host-derived, or self-asserted roots fail closed.
acceptance_tests: WP4 card-chain artifact byte-preservation and non-mutation tests
WP8 card-chain exact hash and linkage tests

rules: Each proof_type has a closed allowlist for generation, lineage, observation_mode, temporal_scope, and execution_outcome; valid enum values cannot be relabelled across proof semantics.
exact_envelope_v3 and native_treasury_execution_v1 are v3 supplemental current accepted proof observed live or by dated snapshot.
safepay_v2 is v2 supplemental current accepted proof observed live or by dated snapshot.

official_x402_settlement_v1 is v3 supplemental current accepted proof observed live or by dated snapshot and requires the full exact-v3 proposal, action, envelope, network, package, contract, and deployment-domain identity.
historical_odra_receipt_v2 is v1 or v2 historical accepted or expected-rejection proof and cannot imply current v3 enforcement.
snapshot is generation none, supplemental, snapshot-observed, not-applicable evidence only.
The official facilitator check name is normalized to facilitator_verify_returned_is_valid_true so it satisfies the frozen snake-case check-name grammar.
Every check observed_at, item captured_at, and registry generated_at is a strict RFC3339 UTC-Z timestamp.
For every verified item, max(check.observed_at) is less than or equal to item captured_at.
Every item captured_at is less than or equal to registry generated_at.
Registry generated_at is less than or equal to the verifier reference time and cannot be trusted as the verifier clock.
Any malformed, reversed, or future chronology makes the affected item or registry invalid rather than green.
acceptance_tests: WP8 future and reversed proof chronology tests

rules: Historical receipt verification consumes raw Casper deploy, canonical-block, state-root, package, and contract JSON-RPC transcripts plus the exact card-chain artifact.
The legacy humanized receipt summary and all asserted passed, processed, or chain_valid booleans are forbidden as verification inputs.

The signed receipt deploy, generation-specific session target, exact ordered 17 typed runtime arguments, terminal card hash, package version, contract ownership, and on-chain Wasm state hash are recomputed and equality-bound to a packaged frozen v1/v2 chain-identity inventory.

The accepted v1 receipt uses StoredContractByHash with the v1 argument order; the accepted v2 receipt uses StoredVersionedContractByHash targeting its package at version 1 with a different signed argument order.

receiptArgumentDigest is SHA-256 of u32-le argument count followed by each exact Casper bytesrepr NamedArg (bytesrepr String name plus bytesrepr CLValue) in signed order; parsed JSON and reordered presentation fields are forbidden preimages.

Offline historical verification has scope artifact_transcript_consistency with no observationSources; embedded raw RPC paths are artifactInputs and cannot prove canonical membership or finality without separate trusted live RPC corroboration.

The canonical exported card chain terminates at the v1 signed root. The v2 receipt signs a different root and cannot be published as a combined receipt-and-card-chain artifact until its own exact chain is independently exported.
Preserved repo source hashes are verified separately and source_deployment_equivalence is unproven; no source-to-deployed-Wasm equivalence claim is allowed.
Missing raw evidence is unavailable and contradictory raw evidence is invalid.
acceptance_tests: generation-specific session variant, target, argument-order, and terminal-root tests
cross-language receipt-argument digest vector and argument-order mutation tests
WP8 historical raw-receipt adapter and forged-summary guard tests

rules: NativeTransferV1 and OfficialX402SettlementV1 use distinct proposal IDs, action IDs, and envelope hashes on the same finalized v3 package, contract, network, and deployment domain.
The canonical public proposal view contains historical, exact-envelope, native-treasury, and SafePay proofs; the official-x402 public proposal view contains only the official settlement proof.

The internal OfficialX402SettlementV1 authorization record is a pre-settlement v3 record and therefore contains no settlement transaction; the public official settlement item binds the later finalized settlement transaction.
The official service compares internal registry package, contract, and deployment-domain fields to a strict finalized-v3 governance config file, never to the separate WCSPR token identity.
Both proposal-scoped public registry documents and the signed-payment internal lookup are independently validated before release.
acceptance_tests: official-x402 governance config and interlock tests
